p1.physical_facts: String of Nickels. Approximately 30+ leaves visible. Leaves are a consistent pale green color. Leaves are densely packed, extending slightly over the pot rim. No visible damage.
p1.explanatory_transformations: Leaf count remains stable across all images. No significant changes in leaf posture or color observed. Appears consistently healthy.
p1.pixel_based_health_reasoning: Consistent coloration and turgor pressure indicate healthy hydration and nutrient uptake. No signs of chlorosis, necrosis, or pest infestation.

p2.physical_facts: Mexican Mint. Two large, wide leaves are present, with a pair of smaller leaves emerging. Leaves are a medium green color. Positioned centrally in the pot. Silver Guest is present near the rim.
p2.explanatory_transformations: The smaller leaves have grown slightly in size over the past 5 days, but the overall growth rate appears slow. No visible wilting or discoloration.
p2.pixel_based_health_reasoning: Leaf color is consistent, suggesting adequate chlorophyll production. No signs of disease or pest damage. Growth appears slightly stunted.

p3.physical_facts: Pothos. Two leaves are present. Leaves are a dark green color. The rabbit toy is positioned near the base of the plant. Leaves are relatively upright.
p3.explanatory_transformations: No new leaf growth observed over the past 5 days. Leaves appear static in position. Slight yellowing at the tips of the leaves is becoming more pronounced.
p3.pixel_based_health_reasoning: The yellowing leaf tips suggest potential nutrient deficiency or overwatering. Lack of new growth indicates stress. The plant appears to be in a state of stasis.

p4.physical_facts: Silver Guest. Small plant near the rim of the pot, sharing space with Mexican Mint. Leaves are small and silvery-green.
p4.explanatory_transformations: Plant size remains relatively constant. No significant changes in leaf color or posture observed. Appears to be growing slowly.
p4.pixel_based_health_reasoning: The silvery-green coloration is typical for this species. No signs of disease or pest damage. Growth appears slow but stable.

visual_health_inference.p3: Stressed, potential nutrient deficiency or overwatering

anomalies: Progressive yellowing of Pothos (p3) leaf tips.
Slow growth rate of Mexican Mint (p2) and Silver Guest (p4).
vision_report.narrative_description: The garden appears generally stable, with the String of Nickels (p1) thriving. The Mexican Mint (p2) and Silver Guest (p4) are growing slowly. The Pothos (p3) is exhibiting signs of stress, indicated by yellowing leaf tips and a lack of new growth. The consistent lighting conditions and lack of direct sunlight are likely contributing to the slow growth rates.
